revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} This template can be used to provide a link to the ''[[:en:Encyclopædia Britannica|Encyclopædia Britannica]]'''s article on a given subject. == Usage == This template may only be used as an inline reference in an article on a related subject. === Parameters === Unnamed parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE | AUTHOR}} Named parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} Combination: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} === Finding the ID === ID is a numerical value given in the html source of the Britannica article in question. {{As of|December 2021}}, it can be seen at "topicId": around line 65 (or just use Ctrl+F, and type in "topicId"). Two ways of viewing the source code on a computer are as follows: * Press Ctrl+U on your computer's keyboard. * [[Right-click]] a blank part of the web page and select '''View page source''' from the [[pop-up menu]] that appears. See for example [https://www.computerhope.com/issues/ch000746.htm How to view the HTML source code of a web page] if you need help with this. The source of https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas says "topicId": 440971 so it would use: {{Britannica|440971}} === Resulting output === It produces a link with the URL https://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/440971/pancreas. It currently redirects to https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas, but that URL may be less stable and cannot be produced by this template. When the template was originally made, Britannica.com included the ID in the normal URL. TITLE provides an optional title for the link in place of the name of the Wikipedia article, in case the Britannica article has a different name for some reason (e.g. a Latin rather than a common name). For example, the Wikipedia article for the wild [[Narcissus (plant)|daffodil]] is titled "''[[Narcissus pseudonarcissus]]''", while Britannica uses [https://www.britannica.com/plant/daffodil "daffodil"] and says "topicId": 149590 in the source. As such, the link would be formatted as follows: {{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} result: :{{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} AUTHOR adds an optional author at the beginning of the output. {{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} result: :{{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} This template places articles in [[:Category:Articles with Encyclopædia Britannica links]]. ==See also== {{Wikidata property|P1417}} *{{tl|Britannica URL}} to cite Britannica using a URL *{{tl|Cite EBO}} to cite Britannica Online using the parameters of {{tlf|Cite encyclopedia}} *{{tl|Cite EB15}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 15th edition *{{tl|Cite EB9}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 9th edition *{{tl|Cite EB1911}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica (1911), in the public domain {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} This template can be used to provide a link to the ''[[:en:Encyclopædia Britannica|Encyclopædia Britannica]]'''s article on a given subject. == Usage == This template may only be used as an inline reference in an article on a related subject. === Parameters === Unnamed parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE | AUTHOR}} Named parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} Combination: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} === Finding the ID === ID is a numerical value given in the html source of the Britannica article in question. {{As of|December 2021}}, it can be seen at "topicId": around line 65 (or just use Ctrl+F, and type in "topicId"). Two ways of viewing the source code on a computer are as follows: * Press Ctrl+U on your computer's keyboard. * [[Right-click]] a blank part of the web page and select '''View page source''' from the [[pop-up menu]] that appears. See for example [https://www.computerhope.com/issues/ch000746.htm How to view the HTML source code of a web page] if you need help with this. The source of https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas says "topicId": 440971 so it would use: {{Britannica|440971}} === Resulting output === It produces a link with the URL https://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/440971/pancreas. It currently redirects to https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas, but that URL may be less stable and cannot be produced by this template. When the template was originally made, Britannica.com included the ID in the normal URL. TITLE provides an optional title for the link in place of the name of the Wikipedia article, in case the Britannica article has a different name for some reason (e.g. a Latin rather than a common name). For example, the Wikipedia article for the wild [[Narcissus (plant)|daffodil]] is titled "''[[Narcissus pseudonarcissus]]''", while Britannica uses [https://www.britannica.com/plant/daffodil "daffodil"] and says "topicId": 149590 in the source. As such, the link would be formatted as follows: {{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} result: :{{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} AUTHOR adds an optional author at the beginning of the output. {{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} result: :{{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} This template places articles in [[:Category:Articles with Encyclopædia Britannica links]]. ==See also== {{Wikidata property|P1417}} *{{tl|Britannica URL}} to cite Britannica using a URL *{{tl|Cite EBO}} to cite Britannica Online using the parameters of {{tlf|Cite encyclopedia}} *{{tl|Cite EB15}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 15th edition *{{tl|Cite EB9}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 9th edition *{{tl|Cite EB1911}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica (1911), in the public domain {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23127601988|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} This template can be used to provide a link to the ''[[:en:Encyclopædia Britannica|Encyclopædia Britannica]]'''s article on a given subject. == Usage == This template may only be used as an inline reference in an article on a related subject. === Parameters === Unnamed parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE | AUTHOR}} Named parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} Combination: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} === Finding the ID === ID is a numerical value given in the html source of the Britannica article in question. {{As of|December 2021}}, it can be seen at "topicId": around line 65 (or just use Ctrl+F, and type in "topicId"). Two ways of viewing the source code on a computer are as follows: * Press Ctrl+U on your computer's keyboard. * [[Right-click]] a blank part of the web page and select '''View page source''' from the [[pop-up menu]] that appears. See for example [https://www.computerhope.com/issues/ch000746.htm How to view the HTML source code of a web page] if you need help with this. The source of https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas says "topicId": 440971 so it would use: {{Britannica|440971}} === Resulting output === It produces a link with the URL https://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/440971/pancreas. It currently redirects to https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas, but that URL may be less stable and cannot be produced by this template. When the template was originally made, Britannica.com included the ID in the normal URL. TITLE provides an optional title for the link in place of the name of the Wikipedia article, in case the Britannica article has a different name for some reason (e.g. a Latin rather than a common name). For example, the Wikipedia article for the wild [[Narcissus (plant)|daffodil]] is titled "''[[Narcissus pseudonarcissus]]''", while Britannica uses [https://www.britannica.com/plant/daffodil "daffodil"] and says "topicId": 149590 in the source. As such, the link would be formatted as follows: {{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} result: :{{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} AUTHOR adds an optional author at the beginning of the output. {{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} result: :{{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} This template places articles in [[:Category:Articles with Encyclopædia Britannica links]]. ==See also== {{Wikidata property|P1417}} *{{tl|Britannica URL}} to cite Britannica using a URL *{{tl|Cite EBO}} to cite Britannica Online using the parameters of {{tlf|Cite encyclopedia}} *{{tl|Cite EB15}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 15th edition *{{tl|Cite EB9}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 9th edition *{{tl|Cite EB1911}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica (1911), in the public domain {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{Documentation subpage}} This template can be used to provide a link to the ''[[:en:Encyclopædia Britannica|Encyclopædia Britannica]]'''s article on a given subject. == Usage == This template may only be used as an inline reference in an article on a related subject. === Parameters === Unnamed parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE | AUTHOR}} Named parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} Combination: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} === Finding the ID === ID is a numerical value given in the html source of the Britannica article in question. {{As of|December 2021}}, it can be seen at "topicId": around line 65 (or just use Ctrl+F, and type in "topicId"). Two ways of viewing the source code on a computer are as follows: * Press Ctrl+U on your computer's keyboard. * [[Right-click]] a blank part of the web page and select '''View page source''' from the [[pop-up menu]] that appears. See for example [https://www.computerhope.com/issues/ch000746.htm How to view the HTML source code of a web page] if you need help with this. The source of https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas says "topicId": 440971 so it would use: {{Britannica|440971}} === Resulting output === It produces a link with the URL https://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/440971/pancreas. It currently redirects to https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas, but that URL may be less stable and cannot be produced by this template. When the template was originally made, Britannica.com included the ID in the normal URL. TITLE provides an optional title for the link in place of the name of the Wikipedia article, in case the Britannica article has a different name for some reason (e.g. a Latin rather than a common name). For example, the Wikipedia article for the wild [[Narcissus (plant)|daffodil]] is titled "''[[Narcissus pseudonarcissus]]''", while Britannica uses [https://www.britannica.com/plant/daffodil "daffodil"] and says "topicId": 149590 in the source. As such, the link would be formatted as follows: {{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} result: :{{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} AUTHOR adds an optional author at the beginning of the output. {{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} result: :{{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} This template places articles in [[:Category:Articles with Encyclopædia Britannica links]]. ==See also== {{Wikidata property|P1417}} *{{tl|Britannica URL}} to cite Britannica using a URL *{{tl|Cite EBO}} to cite Britannica Online using the parameters of {{tlf|Cite encyclopedia}} *{{tl|Cite EB15}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 15th edition *{{tl|Cite EB9}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 9th edition *{{tl|Cite EB1911}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica (1911), in the public domain {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Britannica%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} This template can be used to provide a link to the ''[[:en:Encyclopædia Britannica|Encyclopædia Britannica]]'''s article on a given subject. == Usage == This template may only be used as an inline reference in an article on a related subject. === Parameters === Unnamed parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | TITLE | AUTHOR}} Named parameters: :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE}} :{{tlcw | Britannica | id{{=}}ID | title{{=}}TITLE | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} Combination: :{{tlcw | Britannica | ID | author{{=}}AUTHOR}} === Finding the ID === ID is a numerical value given in the html source of the Britannica article in question. {{As of|December 2021}}, it can be seen at "topicId": around line 65 (or just use Ctrl+F, and type in "topicId"). Two ways of viewing the source code on a computer are as follows: * Press Ctrl+U on your computer's keyboard. * [[Right-click]] a blank part of the web page and select '''View page source''' from the [[pop-up menu]] that appears. See for example [https://www.computerhope.com/issues/ch000746.htm How to view the HTML source code of a web page] if you need help with this. The source of https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas says "topicId": 440971 so it would use: {{Britannica|440971}} === Resulting output === It produces a link with the URL https://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/440971/pancreas. It currently redirects to https://www.britannica.com/science/pancreas, but that URL may be less stable and cannot be produced by this template. When the template was originally made, Britannica.com included the ID in the normal URL. TITLE provides an optional title for the link in place of the name of the Wikipedia article, in case the Britannica article has a different name for some reason (e.g. a Latin rather than a common name). For example, the Wikipedia article for the wild [[Narcissus (plant)|daffodil]] is titled "''[[Narcissus pseudonarcissus]]''", while Britannica uses [https://www.britannica.com/plant/daffodil "daffodil"] and says "topicId": 149590 in the source. As such, the link would be formatted as follows: {{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} result: :{{Britannica|149590|daffodil}} AUTHOR adds an optional author at the beginning of the output. {{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} result: :{{Britannica|440971|pancreas|Robert D. Utiger}} This template places articles in [[:Category:Articles with Encyclopædia Britannica links]]. ==See also== {{Wikidata property|P1417}} *{{tl|Britannica URL}} to cite Britannica using a URL *{{tl|Cite EBO}} to cite Britannica Online using the parameters of {{tlf|Cite encyclopedia}} *{{tl|Cite EB15}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 15th edition *{{tl|Cite EB9}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica, 9th edition *{{tl|Cite EB1911}} to cite Encyclopædia Britannica (1911), in the public domain {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
